name: Mass Polymorph

effects.description: Transform up to ten visible creatures within 120 feet into caster-chosen Beast forms for up to 1 hour with concentration. Unwilling targets make Wisdom saves, unwilling shapechangers automatically succeed, eligible forms must be Beasts the caster has seen within CR or half-level limits, and targets keep Hit Points, alignment, and personality while form statistics, action limits, speech, spellcasting, hands use, gear access, and reversion timing follow the chosen form.

controlOptions.name: Up to ten creatures
controlOptions.effect: The caster transforms up to ten chosen creatures they can see within range.

controlOptions.name: Unwilling save
controlOptions.effect: An unwilling target makes a Wisdom save to resist the transformation.

controlOptions.name: Shapechanger auto success
controlOptions.effect: An unwilling shapechanger automatically succeeds on the save.

controlOptions.name: Beast form choice
controlOptions.effect: Each target assumes a beast form the caster has seen, with CR no higher than the target CR or half the target level.
controlOptions.details: The caster can choose the same beast form or different forms for each target.

controlOptions.name: Stat replacement
controlOptions.effect: The beast form replaces the target game statistics, including mental ability scores, while hit points, alignment, and personality remain.

controlOptions.name: Temporary hit points
controlOptions.effect: Each target gains temporary hit points equal to the new form hit points, and those temporary hit points cannot be replaced by another source.

controlOptions.name: Reversion
controlOptions.effect: A target reverts when it has no temporary hit points, dies, or the spell ends; if the spell ends first, the temporary hit points are lost.

controlOptions.name: Action and communication limits
controlOptions.effect: The creature is limited by the new form and cannot speak, cast spells, or do anything requiring hands or speech.

controlOptions.name: Gear meld
controlOptions.effect: The target gear melds into the new form and cannot be activated, used, wielded, or otherwise provide benefit.

effects.description: Each target gains temporary hit points equal to the hit points of its new beast form; those temporary hit points cannot be replaced by another source.

aiContext.prompt: Ask the caster to choose up to ten target creatures, identify unwilling targets and shapechangers, and choose eligible beast forms the caster has seen for each target.

description: You transform up to ten creatures of your choice that you can see within range. An unwilling target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw to resist the transformation. An unwilling shapechanger automatically succeeds on the save. Each target assumes a beast form of your choice, and you can choose the same form or different ones for each target. The new form can be any beast you have seen whose challenge rating is equal to or less than the target's (or half the target's level, if the target doesn't have a challenge rating). The target's game statistics, including mental ability scores, are replaced by the statistics of the chosen beast, but the target retains its hit points, alignment, and personality. Each target gains a number of temporary hit points equal to the hit points of its new form. These temporary hit points can't be replaced by temporary hit points from another source. A target reverts to its normal form when it has no more temporary hit points or it dies. If the spell ends before then, the creature loses all its temporary hit points and reverts to its normal form. The creature is limited in the actions it can perform by the nature of its new form. It can't speak, cast spells, or do anything else that requires hands or speech. The target's gear melds into the new form. The target can't activate, use, wield, or otherwise benefit from any of its equipment.
